dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6838 IH: To amend the Federal Credit Union Act, the Federal Deposit Insurance Act, the Revised Statutes, and the Federal Reserve Act to require Federal banking agencies to consider economic growth when conducting supervisory functions.
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Barr introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Financial Services

form.official-title: To amend the Federal Credit Union Act, the Federal Deposit Insurance Act, the Revised Statutes, and the Federal Reserve Act to require Federal banking agencies to consider economic growth when conducting supervisory functions.

section.header: Mandate of economic growth in addition to safety and soundness

subsection.text: Section 102 of the Federal Credit Union Act (12 U.S.C. 1752a) is amended by adding at the end the following new subsection:
The Board shall take economic growth into account when conducting supervisory functions under this Act.
Section 1 of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act (12 U.S.C. 1811) is amended by adding at the end the following new subsection:
The Corporation shall take economic growth into account when conducting supervisory functions under this Act.
Section 324(a) of title VII of the Revised Statutes of the United States (12 U.S.C. 1(a)) is amended by striking safety and soundness and inserting safety, soundness, and economic growth.
Section 2A of the Federal Reserve Act (12 U.S.C. 225a) is amended by striking and moderate long-term interest rates. and inserting moderate long-term interest rates, and economic growth..
